assets.name: lecture_01_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: This is the most finished and useful frame: both arrow symbols are complete, the paired \(\sigma=1\) and \(\sigma=-1\) labels are clearly visible, and the board is no longer in the middle of being written. It best captures the notation being introduced for the two spin states.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: and sigma equals minus one by an arrow pointing down

assets.name: lecture_01_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the clearest finished board state for the moment: Susskind is no longer pointing across the diagram, the central up-arrow is fully visible, and the right-hand analyzer sketches are readable. Earlier candidates are more heavily occluded by his arm and hand, especially over the horizontal apparatus drawing.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: but the horizontal component of some vector

assets.name: lecture_01_figure_05.png
assets.rationale: This frame is the most useful for notes because it shows the key mathematics most completely: both the column-vector addition rule and the scalar-multiplication rule are visible at once, and Susskind's hand does not significantly block the expressions. Earlier frames are more occluded, while the later frame crops the right-hand result more tightly.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: And the vector would be a matrix
